title: Object-Oriented Programming I
description: Introduction to programming using Java. Students complete weekly homework assignments covering basic data types, control flow, methods, and object-oriented design. Two midterm exams and a final exam are required.

title: Object-Oriented Programming II
description: Intermediate programming covering data structures and algorithms in Java. Weekly programming projects, two midterms, and a final exam. Students implement linked lists, trees, and hash tables from scratch.

title: Discrete Structures
description: Mathematical foundations of computer science including logic, proof techniques, sets, relations, and graph theory. Weekly problem sets, two midterm exams, and a comprehensive final.

title: Organization of Programming Languages
description: Study of programming language design and implementation. Weekly programming projects in OCaml, Rust, and Ruby. Two midterms and a final exam.

title: Algorithms
description: Rigorous analysis of algorithms and complexity theory. Weekly problem sets, a midterm, and a final exam. Demands strong mathematical background and extensive written proofs.

title: Computer and Network Security
description: Study of security vulnerabilities, cryptography, and secure system design. Several security projects, weekly homework, two midterms.

title: Data Structures
description: Advanced data structures and their applications. Weekly programming assignments using Java. Midterm exam, final project, and weekly quizzes.

title: Introduction to Engineering Design
description: Project-based introduction to engineering. Weekly lab sessions, design reports, two midterms, and a final design showcase.

title: Mechanics of Materials
description: Stress, strain, and material deformation. Weekly homework, lab reports, two midterm exams, and a comprehensive final.

title: Elements of Discrete Signal Analysis
description: Mathematical treatment of discrete-time signals and systems. Weekly problem sets, two midterm exams, and a comprehensive final. Demanding mathematical content.

title: Electromagnetic Theory
description: Electrostatics, magnetostatics, and electromagnetic waves. Weekly problem sets, MATLAB assignments, two exams, and a design project.

title: Calculus I
description: Introduction to differential calculus. Weekly homework assignments, two midterm exams and a final. Covers limits, derivatives, and applications.

title: Calculus II
description: Integral calculus and infinite series. Weekly homework, quizzes every two weeks, two midterms and a final. Faster pace than Calculus I.

title: Introduction to Linear Algebra
description: Vectors, matrices, linear systems, and eigenvalues. Weekly homework assignments, two midterms and a final. Emphasis on both computation and theory.

title: Calculus III
description: Multivariable calculus including partial derivatives, multiple integrals, and vector calculus. Weekly homework, two midterms and a final.

title: Advanced Calculus I
description: Rigorous treatment of calculus including epsilon-delta proofs. Weekly proof-based homework, two midterms, final exam. One of the most challenging undergraduate math courses.

title: Advanced Calculus II
description: Continuation of real analysis covering sequences of functions and metric spaces. Weekly problem sets with proofs, midterm and final exam.

title: Chemistry I — Fundamentals
description: Introduction to chemistry including atomic structure and stoichiometry. Weekly problem sets, lab reports, two midterms and a final.

title: Organic Chemistry I
description: Mechanistic organic chemistry. Notorious for demanding workload: weekly homework, lab reports every week, two midterms and a final. Lab takes 3 hours per session.

title: Principles of Biology I
description: Molecular and cellular biology concepts. Weekly reading assignments, two exams, lab reports, and a final exam.

title: Genetics
description: Classical and molecular genetics. Weekly problem sets, two midterms, final, and a research paper.

title: General Physics — Mechanics
description: Mechanics and thermodynamics. Weekly homework, recitation quizzes, two midterms and a final. Lab each week with report due.

title: General Physics — Vibrations Waves Optics and Thermodynamics
description: Wave phenomena, optics, and thermodynamics. Weekly homework, lab reports, two midterms and a cumulative final.

title: Principles of Micro Economics
description: Introduction to microeconomic theory and markets. Three exams and weekly reading; minimal homework assignments.

title: Intermediate Macroeconomic Theory and Policy
description: Mathematical treatment of macroeconomic theory. Weekly problem sets, two midterms and a final.

title: Introduction to Psychology
description: Survey of major areas of psychology. Two exams, short weekly reflections, and weekly reading quizzes.

title: Developmental Psychology
description: Survey of human development from birth through adulthood. Two short papers, two exams, and weekly reading quizzes.

title: Research Methods in Psychology
description: Statistical methods and experimental design. Weekly assignments, midterm, final, and a research project with written report.

title: History of the United States to 1865
description: Survey of American history to the Civil War. Three essays, two exams, and weekly reading.

title: Modern Europe
description: Advanced reading-intensive seminar. Weekly response papers, a long research paper, and participation in discussion.

title: Academic Writing
description: Introduction to academic writing. Four major essays and multiple drafts required. Weekly writing workshops.

title: Advanced Academic Writing
description: Close reading and analysis of canonical American texts. Weekly responses, two essays, midterm and final.

title: Introduction to Physical Fitness and Health
description: Beginner fitness course. Pass/fail. Attendance is the primary requirement; no exams or written assignments.

title: Exercise Physiology
description: Physiology of physical activity and exercise. Lab assignments, two midterms, final, and a fitness project.

title: Music Theory
description: Fundamentals of music theory and composition. Weekly exercises, two exams, and a composition project.

title: Introduction to Drawing
description: Studio art course focused on observational drawing. Weekly studio assignments and a portfolio review at end of semester.

title: Advanced Painting
description: Upper-division studio art. Weekly critiques, project proposals, and a final portfolio. Requires significant out-of-class studio time.

title: Foundations of Community Health Nursing
description: Introduction to public health nursing concepts. Two exams, short weekly reflections, and a community assessment project.

title: Advanced Clinical Practicum
description: Advanced clinical nursing practice. Extensive clinical hours, weekly reflective journals, case study presentations, and a comprehensive final portfolio.

title: Applied Probability and Statistics I
description: Probability theory, distributions, and inference. Weekly problem sets, two midterms and a final exam.

title: Introduction to Probability Theory
description: Advanced statistical theory and estimation. Weekly problem sets with proofs, midterm and final exam.

title: Oral Communication
description: Survey of communication theory and practice. Two exams and a short presentation; minimal weekly work.

title: Media and Society
description: Reading-heavy seminar with weekly reaction posts, a midterm exam, and a final investigative journalism project.

title: Introduction to Philosophy
description: Survey of major philosophical problems and thinkers. Weekly readings, two take-home essays, and a final exam.

title: Epistemology
description: Advanced seminar on theories of knowledge. Weekly response papers, a midterm essay, and a long final paper.

title: Object-Oriented Programming for Information Science
description: Object-oriented programming in Python applied to information problems. Weekly programming assignments, two midterms, and a final project.

title: Big Data and Machine Learning
description: Machine learning methods for large datasets. Demanding weekly programming assignments, midterm exam, and a semester-long data science project.
